Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Jerry M. Parker

RULING:
DEFENDANT FOOTHILL HARDWARE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Foothill Hardware, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant has not satisfied its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that plaintiff was not exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Mr. Wellington's deposition testimony and Dowman's GO 129 interrogatory responses preclude defendant's reliance on the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
Defendant Ashby Lumber Company's motion for summary judgment. No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. Liberally construed, plaintiff's deposition testimony that he breathed visible dust from "asbestos tape" purchased from Ashby Lumber is sufficient to create a triable issue regarding exposure to an asbestos-containing product attributable to Ashby Lumber. The Court declined ruling on defendant's objections. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Maria Lertora Aliventi, CSR # 11935

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-01-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FLUOR CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Appearances:
Jeffrey Wehr of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion is denied. It is premised on demonstrating that defendant was not present at the Standard Oil/Chevron Refinery in Richmond. However, Mr. Smith's Declaration fails to meet the requirement of Evidence Code 1272 that the records reviewed were made in the regular course of business and are trustworthy. As a consequence, defendant has failed to shift the burden of proof. Assuming the burden was shifted, the declarations of Parker, Mingram and Ay construed in favor of plaintiff are sufficient to create a triable issue of fact. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225
